title: Finding
description: Machine-readable schema for the finding-format.md block shape. The merger validates every reviewer-emitted finding against this; malformed findings are silently discarded.

severity.description: P0 must fix (blocks merge); P1 should fix before merge; P2 should fix soon; P3 nice to have. Default to P2 when uncertain.

category.description: Drives sensitivity gating. category=security forces Opus dispatch regardless of file path.

file.description: Repo-relative path. Must not start with whitespace or absolute slash. For aggregate findings, use the first file in files_affected (or '.' for repo-wide).

line.description: Single integer line number — the line where the problem is most acute. For aggregate findings, set to 1.

summary.description: One sentence, declarative. No leading or trailing whitespace.

why.description: One sentence on the root cause or concrete consequence.

fix.description: One sentence on the change. Prefix conventions: 'decide:' routes to judgment_findings (never auto-applied, escalated to user); 'auto: <command>' runs the named command via fix-applier without LLM editing (used by review-hygiene for linter --fix runs); anything else is a concrete mechanical edit the fix-applier translates into a Edit-tool call.

kind.description: individual = one finding per violation (default). aggregate = one finding that represents N violations the named tool can fix in bulk; orchestrator/agent context never sees the per-violation detail, only the aggregate count. Used by review-hygiene to keep large lint/format runs out of the orchestrator's context.

tool.description: For aggregate findings: the tool that produced the violations and can fix them (e.g. 'prettier', 'eslint', 'biome', 'gofmt', 'ruff'). Optional for individual findings.

files_affected.description: For aggregate findings: list of files the aggregate covers. Used by the merger to partition packets and by fix-applier to know which paths to run the tool on. Optional for individual findings.

files_affected_count.description: For aggregate findings: count of files covered. Surfaced in the report so the user sees scale without the merger having to compute it.

violations_count.description: For aggregate findings: count of individual violations the aggregate represents. Surfaced in the report.

slice.description: Optional slice name, stamped by the merger when running per-slice.

reviewer.description: Optional reviewer identifier, stamped during schema parsing.

evidence.description: Optional — used by review-ux for screenshot paths and similar attachments.

originating_reviewers.description: Optional — populated by the merger after dedup to list every reviewer that contributed to this finding.

apply_validated.description: Optional — set by the merger to false when a fix is too vague for git apply --check construction but the finding is still kept.
